title: UPI OC 134 Adherence to RBI PPI Interoperability Guidelines
text:
NEED

NATIONAL PAYMENTS CORPORATION OF INDIA

NPCVUPIOC No. 134/2021-22 315 January, 2022

To

PPI Members - RBI PPI Interoperability

MadanVSir,

Subject: Adherence to RBI PPI Interoperability guidelines by 31st March, 2022

As per the RBI Master Directions on Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPls) - RBI/DPSS/2021-22/82
(Updated as on 12" November, 2021) regarding Issuance and Operation of Prepaid Payment
Instruments, it shall be mandatory to give holders of full KYC compliant PPIs interoperability through
UPI for the PPls (in the form of wallets) by 31 March 2022. This is pursuant to PPI guidelines issued
on October 11, 2017 on Issuance and Operation of Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPI-MD).

Interoperability guidelines for PPI Issuers through UPI

PPI issuer shall facilitate all basic / standard features of interoperability of UP!

PPI issuer shall act as Payment System Providers (PSP) in UPI. NPC! shall issue handle to
the PP! issuer as per policy / guidelines taking risk management aspects into consideration.
PPI holders shall be on-boarded for UP] by their own PPI issuer only. PPI issuer shall only link
its customer wallets to the handle issued to it.

Authentication shall be completed by the PPI holder as per existing wallet credentials.

For the purposes of settlement, a non-bank PPI issuer shall participate through a sponsor
bank. Non-bank PPI issuer shall adhere to the tequirements of sponsor bank arrangement in
UP! and also meet all relevant requirements of NPCI.

Directives

PPI issuers are requested to go live for PP! on UPI and ensure compliance to the RBI PPI
Interoperability guidelines by 31st March, 2022.

Yours sincerely,

SD/-

Kunal Kalawatia
Chief Products

10014, The Capital, B Wing, 10% Floor,

Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra ¢E), Mumbai 400 051.
